title: QUIC RFC 9001 Non-Retained Stateless Reset Suppression Work Item
summary: Implement and prove the suppressive no-retained-route/no-token stateless-reset matrix cell after AEAD-limit terminal discard.
planned_changes:
- Keep packets that do not resolve to a retained route suppressive instead of advancing them toward stateless-reset emission.
- Keep packets that resolve only to route state without a remembered token suppressive instead of fabricating a response datagram.
- Keep the positive retained-route floor from `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0006` separate from this suppressive topoff.
- Add requirement-home coverage for unknown-route suppression, missing-token suppression, a retained-route boundary check, and deterministic fuzz coverage over route/token presence.
- Keep the permanent `QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks` suite as the hot-path evidence source for retained-route lookup miss and no-token miss paths.
verification_plan: Run the focused REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0009 requirement-home tests, keep the retained-route, malformed-formatting, and known-reset matrix guards green, refresh QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks in Dry mode, render the touched SpecTrace markdown views from JSON, and finish with git diff --check.
completion_notes: Completed as the suppressive no-retained-route/no-token topoff after the proof landed. The retained-route positive floor and known-token loop-suppression cell remain separate slices.
